name: University of Cambridge
notes: Cambridge students apply to Engineering (H100); electrical and information engineering specialisation is chosen from Year 3. ENGAA required; interview at college. World-class signal processing, power electronics and semiconductor research groups.

name: Imperial College London
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Imperial EEE is internationally renowned for control systems, digital communications and power electronics. Strong employer links to Siemens, National Grid, ARM and major tech companies. A* in Maths expected; Physics required.

name: University of Bristol
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bristol EEE has strong photonics, wireless communications and advanced signal processing research. Industry links to Rolls-Royce and BAE Systems. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Southampton
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Southampton is a world leader in photonics, optical fibre and microelectronics — spinouts from the university supply major telecoms companies globally. The Optoelectronics Research Centre is internationally recognised. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Edinburgh
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match (also offers standalone BEng/MEng Electrical Engineering). Edinburgh's electronics group is strong in microelectronics, photonics and power systems. 4-year Scottish BEng or 5-year MEng. Mathematics required.

name: University of Manchester
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Manchester EEE benefits from graphene and advanced materials research (National Graphene Institute). Strong power electronics and renewable energy groups. Mathematics required; Physics recommended.

name: University of Warwick
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Warwick EE has strong embedded systems, automotive electronics and power electronics groups; WMG (Warwick Manufacturing Group) is a key industry link for EV and autonomous systems research. Mathematics required.

name: University of Bath
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bath EEE consistently achieves exceptional graduate employment through the mandatory placement (Semester 5). Placement employers include Airbus, Thales, BAE Systems and Dyson. Mathematics required; Physics recommended.

name: University of Nottingham
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Nottingham EEE is strong in power electronics, wireless communications and photovoltaic research. George Green Institute for Electromagnetics Research is nationally recognised. Mathematics required.

name: University of Sheffield
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Sheffield EEE has strong power systems, control and smart grid research. Collaboration with National Grid on UK grid decarbonisation. The Solar 21 research centre focuses on photovoltaics and energy storage.

name: University of Leeds
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Leeds EEE has good renewable energy and smart systems research. Industrial placement year available. Mathematics required at A-level.

name: Loughborough University
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Loughborough EEE has strong motorsport electronics and automotive applications links. Excellent industry placement culture. Mathematics required.
